chapter_context.summary: Presents the sign of Immanuel given to the fearful King Ahaz amid the threats of Syria and Ephraim, prophesying the ultimate deliverance and subsequent Assyrian invasion.

outline.text: Reassuring King Ahaz Against the Threat of Syria and Israel
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Reassuring King Ahaz Against the Threat of Syria and Israel (7:1-9), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.
This section focuses on The Promising Sign of Immanuel (7:10-17), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: The Impending Assyrian Invasion and Agricultural Desolation
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Impending Assyrian Invasion and Agricultural Desolation (7:18-25), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Judgment
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of judgment, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Holy One of Israel
key_themes.description: Isaiah's signature title for God, highlighting His absolute transcendent holiness, moral purity, and unique relationship with His people.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and promises of Chapter 7, how does God's double character of absolute holiness and deep comfort speak to your life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to trust in God's promises of ultimate renewal and restoration in times of spiritual or physical exile?

cultural_historical.title: The Role of the Prophet in Israel
cultural_historical.text: Prophets in the Hebrew Bible were not just fortune-tellers, but 'covenant enforcement agents'—called to confront sin, demand social justice, and bring hope of future restoration.
